notes: BG 2.13 is the analogical proof of Self-eternality, used by all major commentarial traditions. The engineering layer remains at the level of the analogical structure (state transitions don't collapse identity) without extending into the metaphysical claim about reincarnation that the Sanskrit explicitly makes. This is a tasteful restraint: the verse's full claim includes 'so it passes to another body', which is a doctrinal commitment the engineering layer wisely does not try to translate. Instead, it works the analogical structure faithfully (v1 → v2 → v3 → v4 of a service) and assigns the appropriate engineering predicate (conformance test suite). No tradition is conflated, because the engineering layer makes no claim that requires tradition-naming. The dhīra is preserved with technical specificity (steady-minded, not 'wise in the abstract'), which Shankara, Ramanuja, and the literal reading all accept.
